name: Union Bank And Trust Company
description_short: Union Bank & Trust is a union-owned bank specializing in custody and trust services for Taft-Hartley pension plans, institutional clients, and individuals, managing over $12 billion in assets.
description_long:
Union Bank & Trust was established to serve the union movement and has operated for nearly 50 years as a specialized custodian for employee benefit plans and institutional clients. The bank is union-owned and staffed by members of OPEIU Local 12, distinguishing it as a genuinely union-aligned financial institution rather than a mainstream bank with union-friendly marketing.

The company offers comprehensive custody and trust services including Taft-Hartley pension plan administration, collective investment vehicles, public pension fund custody, self-directed IRAs with alternative assets (real estate, etc.), 401(k) plans, and corporate investment accounts. They also provide commercial banking services such as treasury management, payment processing, fraud prevention, and positive pay systems. Additionally, they offer checking and savings accounts specifically designed for union organizations, including Union Advantage Checking and Union Plus Savings accounts that emphasize competitive returns and accessibility.

Union Bank & Trust distinguishes itself through deep expertise in Taft-Hartley plans, having originally been established to serve this specific market segment. They maintain nearly 50 years of specialized experience in pension and employee benefit custody, employ cutting-edge technology for custody operations, and explicitly market themselves as an alternative to "anti-union pricing" found at larger institutions. Their union ownership model and commitment to reinvesting profits into union movements appeals to values-aligned organizations.

The company appears legitimate and well-established, managing substantial assets ($12 billion+) and operating within regulated banking frameworks. However, they focus primarily on institutional and high-net-worth clients rather than consumer retail banking, which limits their applicability to typical individual consumers seeking basic banking products. Their specialized Taft-Hartley expertise means they are optimally positioned for pension plans and union-affiliated organizations rather than general-purpose personal finance. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Taft-Hartley pension plan custody and administration
Self-directed IRA custody including alternative assets
Corporate investment account and trust custody
Fraud prevention and positive pay services
pros: Manages over $12 billion in client assets with 50 years of specialized Taft-Hartley pension plan experience
Union-owned and staffed by union members (OPEIU Local 12), ensuring alignment with union values
Offers self-directed IRA custody with alternative assets including real estate and non-traditional investments
Provides competitive treasury management and fraud prevention tools at union-friendly pricing without anti-union markups
Expertise in institutional custody across multiple account types (401k plans, collective investment vehicles, public pensions)
Union Advantage Checking and Union Plus Savings accounts designed specifically for union organizations with competitive returns
Accessible contact options (phone: 612-437-5207 or 612-436-1424; email: customerservice@ubtmn.com)
cons: Primarily targets institutional clients and Taft-Hartley plans; not positioned for average individual consumers
Minimal information about personal banking fees, interest rates, or account minimums on their website
No information about FDIC insurance limits, account protections, or regulatory certifications prominently displayed
Limited retail banking features compared to mainstream banks; no mention of mobile banking, online account opening, or consumer lending
best_for: Taft-Hartley pension plans and union-affiliated employee benefit programs seeking specialized custody services
Union organizations and labor groups looking for banking that reinvests profits into the union movement
Institutional clients requiring custody for collective investment vehicles or public pension fund administration
Self-directed IRA investors seeking alternative asset custody (real estate, non-traditional investments)
diagnosis: Union Bank & Trust is best suited for institutional clients, pension plans, union organizations, and high-net-worth individuals seeking specialized custody services with union values alignment, not for typical retail banking consumers. The primary caveat is that they are an institutional-focused bank with deep expertise in a specific niche (Taft-Hartley plans) rather than a general-purpose consumer bank offering standard checking/savings to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
